headline: ConocoPhillips and Loar added to Goldman Sachs conviction list
summary: Goldman Sachs has shaken up its US “conviction list – directors’ cut”, adding oil major ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP, XETRA:YCP) and aerospace supplier Loar Holdings (NYSE:LOAR), while removing Huntington Ingalls Industries Inc (NYSE:HII), Valero Energy Corp (NYSE:VLO) and Madison Square Garden...

headline: Exxon Is Down Since the Iran Conflict. These Energy Stocks Are Bigger Winners.
summary: For all the drama in oil markets since the recent conflict in Iran began, stocks of U.S. oil majors such as  Exxon  Mobil—and even those of many oil producers—haven’t budged much.  In fact, Exxon stock is down from its Friday closing price.  The markets for the fuels those companies produce have been even more volatile than the overall oil market.

headline: Valero Energy Corporation to Announce First Quarter 2026 Earnings Results on April 30, 2026
summary: SAN ANTONIO, March 03, 2026--Valero Energy Corporation (NYSE: VLO) announced today that it will host a conference call on Thursday, April 30, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. ET to discuss its financial and operational results for the first quarter of 2026. The earnings release will be issued earlier that morning.

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Valero Energy (VLO) After Its Recent Share Price Surge
summary: If you are wondering whether Valero Energy's share price still offers value after a strong run, this article walks through what the current numbers actually say about the stock. The shares last closed at US$214.91, with returns of 8.9% over 7 days, 18.5% over 30 days, 30.0% year to date, 72.3% over 1 year and 217.2% over 5 years. This performance has sharpened the focus on what is already priced in. Recent coverage around Valero Energy has highlighted its role as a large US refiner and fuel...

headline: Reddit Is Bullish on XLE But Crude Oil Tells a Different Story
summary: The State Street Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLE), is up 27% year-to-date and 13% over the past month, trading at $56.78 Monday morning. The catalyst is unmistakable: Iran-Israel tensions have escalated sharply, with the U.S. ordering non-essential embassy staff out of Israel, a development the world is keeping a close eye on. All the ... Reddit Is Bullish on XLE But Crude Oil Tells a Different Story
